what_worked_well: ls topics/ enumerated topics as plain directories: returned 'control' and 'weather' exactly as DEMO.md Beat 4 claims. No admin CLI.

ls topics/weather/sub_*.fifo | wc -l returned the correct live subscriber count (weather=2, control=1) using the verbatim command from DEMO.md Beat 4. Per-sub FIFOs are real named pipes (prw-r--r-- sub_56.fifo / sub_57.fifo) -- the topic dir IS the subscriber registry.
Deferred messages are visible via ls deferred/ as <run_at_ms>.<seq> filenames (e.g. 1782179331849.321228761); ls deferred/ | sort | head -1 gives the next deadline exactly as documented.

BONUS over spec: a pending deferred file is fully cat-able -- cat showed 'control' then 'reboot-later' (topic on line 1, payload after). The challenge only asked to LIST pending deferred messages; an operator can also READ the target topic and payload of a scheduled message with plain cat. Free win.
Deferred delivery fired on time: the control subscriber received 'reboot-later' after --delay 8, and deferred/ emptied afterward -- fire-once is observable directly in the filesystem.
The whole state dir is transparent under ls -la $D: deferred/, outbox/, topics/, wake.fifo, the unix socket. The FS genuinely is the admin interface; nothing hidden.

Shipped tests/introspection.sh passed all 7 checks (I1 topics-as-dirs, I2 ls sub_*.fifo count, I3 drops_<pid> cat-able counter=7, I4 deferred visible via ls pending=1 next=<ts>, I5 GC reaper prunes stale drops_* and empty topic dirs while preserving live state). This is the canonical proof of every introspection verb my challenge asked for.

drops_* IS a plain cat-able counter when a real overflow occurs: shipped flood_wedged.sh reported sum drops_*=344 and introspection.sh I3 showed drops_<pid>=7 -- visible, never silent, exactly as DEMO.md Beat 3 promises.
wished_for: A zero-initialized drops_<pid> file per subscriber (or a documented note that absence == zero drops), so an operator running the literal DEMO.md line `cat topics/$T/drops_*` on a healthy topic sees `0` or empty output instead of a glob-no-match error.
A one-line `ls`-friendly index or README snippet in the state dir (e.g. a tiny 'layout.txt') so a new operator doesn't have to discover deferred/ vs outbox/ vs topics/ by trial.
DEMO.md Beat 4 could show the deferred file is cat-able (topic+payload), since that is a genuinely nice introspection feature it currently undersells.
evidence_log: [B] `ls $D/topics/weather/sub_*.fifo | wc -l` -> 2 ; control -> 1 (verbatim DEMO.md Beat 4 command, correct counts).
[C] `ls -la $D/topics/weather/` -> prw-r--r-- sub_56.fifo, prw-r--r-- sub_57.fifo, -rw-r--r-- .drops.lock (FIFOs are real named pipes).
fan-out: w1.out='sunny', w2.out='sunny' after one `pub weather` -> every live sub got it.
[F bonus] `cat $D/deferred/1782179331849.321228761` -> 'control' then 'reboot-later' (topic line 1, payload after; 20 bytes).
[deferred fired] after --delay 8, `cat $D/c1.out` -> 'reboot-later' and `ls $D/deferred/` is empty (fire-once observable).
[G] `ls -la $D` -> broker.log, deferred/, outbox/, s.sock (socket), topics/, wake.fifo (named pipe) -- whole state visible.
Shipped: `bash tests/introspection.sh` -> pass=7 fail=0 ; I3 'drops_<pid> is a plain cat-able counter (=7)'; I4 'deferred queue visible via ls (pending=1, next=1782179662692.726516760)'.
Shipped: `bash tests/flood_wedged.sh` (2nd run) -> pass=4 fail=0, 'wedged-sub overflow is visible (sum drops_*=344)', max_descendants=17, completed in <90s.

FRICTION: `bash tests/flood_wedged.sh` (1st run, fresh container) printed only its banner '== flood_wedged: src/shellmux ==' then was SIGKILLed at the 120s timeout (EXIT=137). DEMO.md Beat 3 claims '~5s'. 2nd run passed normally -> flaky/slow on cold start.

FRICTION: on a HEALTHY topic, `cat $D/topics/weather/drops_*` (literal DEMO.md Beat 4 line) -> `cat: '.../drops_*': No such file or directory`, exit 1. drops_<pid> is created lazily only after the first drop; the doc presents the cat as if it always works.

friction_points.description: DEMO.md Beat 4 lists `cat $D/topics/control/drops_*` as a flat introspection command, but on a healthy topic (no subscriber has overflowed yet) the drops_<pid> file does not exist, so the literal command errors: `cat: '.../drops_*': No such file or directory` (exit 1). An operator following the doc on a working broker sees an error, not a `0` or empty output. drops_<pid> is created lazily on first drop.
friction_points.evidence: Ran the literal line on a healthy weather topic with 1 sub: `cat $D/topics/weather/drops_* 2>&1` -> `cat: '.../drops_*': No such file or directory`, exit 1. ls of the same glob also reports No such file or directory.
friction_points.workaround: Use `cat $D/topics/$T/drops_* 2>/dev/null` or treat absence-of-file as zero drops; or trigger a real overflow first (then the counter appears and cats cleanly, as introspection.sh I3 confirms).

friction_points.description: Shipped tests/flood_wedged.sh (the operator-facing command DEMO.md Beat 3 tells you to run, claimed '~5s') hung on a cold-start container: it printed only its banner and was killed at the 120s timeout (EXIT=137). A retry completed cleanly in <90s with pass=4 fail=0, drops=344. Large run-to-run variance and a >120s worst case vs a documented '~5s' is misleading and would frustrate an operator validating backpressure.
friction_points.evidence: Run 1 (fresh container): output = only '== flood_wedged: src/shellmux ==', timed out, EXIT=137. Run 2: full output, 'sum drops_*=344', max_descendants=17, '== flood_wedged result: pass=4 fail=0 ==', EXIT=0, well under 90s.
friction_points.workaround: Re-run the suite; it appears to pass reliably once warm. But the '~5s' claim in DEMO.md should be qualified (e.g. 'up to ~2m on a cold/loaded host').

friction_points.description: As a hands-on operator I could not make a subscriber 'wedge' on my own to surface a drops_<pid> file: SIGSTOP on the `sub` client process and a raw `socat ... SYSTEM:'sleep 600'` reader both failed to provoke ring overflow even under ~2900-4000 flooded messages. Only the shipped harness's specific wedge mechanism reliably produces drops. This means an operator wanting to *see* the drop-counter feature live has no obvious documented way to trigger it outside the test.
friction_points.evidence: Two manual scenarios (SIGSTOP-ed sub, raw-socat sleep reader) flooded thousands of messages but `ls topics/weather/drops_*` stayed empty; only tests/flood_wedged.sh and tests/introspection.sh produced drops_* (344 and 7 respectively).
friction_points.workaround: Trust the shipped tests for the drops demonstration; the counter file is genuinely cat-able once a real overflow occurs.

free_text:
CHALLENGE OUTCOME: PASS. I enumerated topics, counted subscribers per topic, read drop counters, and listed pending deferred messages using only ls and cat -- zero bespoke admin commands. All four success criteria are met and I verified each from real container output, not source (I did not read src/).

- ls topics/ -> control, weather (directories). PASS.
- ls topics/$T/sub_*.fifo | wc -l -> weather=2, control=1 (correct, verbatim DEMO.md command). PASS.
- cat topics/$T/drops_* -> a plain integer counter once an overflow exists (introspection.sh I3=7; flood_wedged sum=344). PASS, with the caveat in FP1 that the file is lazily created so the literal cat errors on a healthy topic.
- ls deferred/ | sort -> <run_at_ms>.<seq> filenames; head -1 = next deadline. PASS. Bonus: the deferred file is fully cat-able (topic + payload), which the docs undersell.

The 'state IS the filesystem' design is the real strength here -- ls -la $D shows the entire broker (topics/, deferred/, outbox/, wake.fifo, the socket) and there is no protocol to learn. The shipped tests/introspection.sh (pass=7) is effectively a spec-of-my-challenge and it green-passes every verb.

Two medium frictions worth fixing before a homelab operator hits them: (FP1) DEMO.md's literal `cat .../drops_*` errors on the common healthy case because drop counters are created lazily -- either zero-init them or document 'no file == zero drops'; and (FP2) the flood_wedged.sh suite that DEMO.md tells operators to run and calls '~5s' actually hung >120s on a cold container before passing cleanly on retry -- the timing claim is not honest about cold-start/worst-case and the run-to-run variance is large. Neither affects my challenge's pass, but both directly hit this persona's expectations ('introspection via plain tools', 'no surprises').

NOTE on scope/honesty: this challenge does not touch the deadline proof, but I incidentally observed a clean deferred fire (--delay 8 landed on the control sub, deferred/ emptied) consistent with the headline claim. I did NOT independently re-run the 5000-trial chaos gate or audit its negative control in this session, so I make no judgement on missed=0/dup=0 here.

proof_assessment.incidental_deferred_observation: A --delay 8 publish to 'control' fired on time (subscriber received 'reboot-later') and the deferred/ file (1782179331849.321228761) was consumed/removed -- consistent with fire-once, but NOT a re-run of the 5000-trial chaos gate or its must-fail negative control.
proof_assessment.introspection_claims_audited: All four challenge criteria + DEMO.md Beat 4 commands audited against live container output; 3 of 4 work verbatim, the drops_* cat errors on a healthy topic (FP1).

claim_audit.evidence: weather=2, control=1; FIFOs are real named pipes

Returns an integer once a drop has occurred (=7 in introspection.sh, sum=344 in flood). But on a healthy topic the file does not exist and the literal cat errors (exit 1). Lazily created; doc does not say so. See FP1.
ls deferred/ -> 1782179331849.321228761 (<run_at_ms>.<seq>); sorts to the next deadline; file is also cat-able for topic+payload
drops ARE visible (sum=344) but the run took >120s on a cold container (timed out at EXIT=137) before passing cleanly on retry. The '~5s' timing is not honest about cold/worst case. See FP2.

claim_audit.claim_source: DEMO.md Beat 4: 'No admin protocol. State IS the filesystem'
claim_audit.evidence: Enumerated topics, sub counts, drops, and deferred queue entirely with ls/cat; ls -la $D exposes the whole broker

errors_encountered.error: background container task killed before completing
errors_encountered.cause: I flooded with 500 SEQUENTIAL `pub` calls, each of which BLOCKS for its linger -- far too slow, exceeded the wait window. My test design flaw, not a broker bug.
errors_encountered.resolution: Rewrote to pipe many lines through a single pub and to background parallel publishers.

errors_encountered.error: EXIT=137 (SIGKILL at 120s timeout), only banner printed
errors_encountered.cause: Suite hung on cold-start container; passed on retry. Logged as FP2.
errors_encountered.resolution: Re-ran; second run passed pass=4 fail=0.

errors_encountered.error: no drops_* file ever appeared despite thousands of messages
errors_encountered.cause: SIGSTOP on the sub client / raw-socat sleep-reader did not trigger the broker's bounded-ring overflow path. Logged as FP3.
errors_encountered.resolution: Used shipped flood_wedged.sh + introspection.sh to confirm drops_* is real and cat-able.
